keyMyths.title: The Covenant of the Cut
keyMyths.description: Azrael's Eyes, a young initiate, undergoes ritual circumcision, grappling with the ancient Egyptian parallels of purity and the emerging Israelite covenant, ultimately accepting his role in the lineage of Abraham.

keyMyths.title: The Desert Vision
keyMyths.description: While wandering in the desert, Azrael's Eyes receives a prophetic vision, granting him insight into the future struggles and triumphs of the Israelites, and the importance of maintaining the covenant through circumcision.

keyMyths.title: The Trial of the Uncircumcised
keyMyths.description: Azrael's Eyes confronts a community that has abandoned the practice of circumcision, engaging in theological debates and demonstrating the spiritual necessity of the ritual to uphold their connection with God.

keyMyths.title: The Healing Waters of the Nile
keyMyths.description: During a famine, Azrael's Eyes travels to Egypt, where he uses his knowledge of both Egyptian purification rituals and the Israelite covenant to purify the Nile River, bringing forth sustenance for both Israelites and Egyptians.

keyMyths.title: The Defense of the Temple Mount
keyMyths.description: When invaders threaten to defile the Temple Mount, Azrael's Eyes, empowered by his unwavering faith in the covenant, leads a small band of warriors in a desperate defense, ultimately securing the sanctity of the Temple.

achievements: Championed the practice of circumcision as a cornerstone of the Israelite covenant.
Bridged the gap between Egyptian and Israelite understandings of ritual purity.
Successfully defended the Temple Mount against desecration.
Received prophetic visions guiding the Israelites through times of crisis.
Revived the practice of circumcision in communities that had abandoned it.
Purified the Nile River, bringing sustenance during a famine.
Interpreted ancient texts to reveal the spiritual significance of the covenant.
